item.name: Amazon Redshift Get the Results of a Sql Statement

request.name: Amazon Redshift Get the Results of a Sql Statement

description.content: Fetches the result of a SQL statement that was submitted with ExecuteStatement or BatchExecuteStatement. The statement must have completed successfully (status FINISHED). Results are paginated and the maximum result size is 100 MB. Use the NextToken parameter to retrieve subsequent pages.

variable.description: AWS region identifier (This can only be one of us-east-1,us-east-2,us-west-1,us-west-2,eu-west-1,eu-west-2,eu-central-1,ap-southeast-1,ap-southeast-2,ap-northeast-1,ap-northeast-2,ca-central-1,sa-east-1)

Execute SQL statements against Amazon Redshift clusters or serverless workgroups

item.name: Amazon Redshift Execute a Sql Statement

request.name: Amazon Redshift Execute a Sql Statement

description.content: Runs a SQL statement against an Amazon Redshift cluster or Amazon Redshift Serverless workgroup. The SQL statement runs asynchronously and returns a statement identifier that you can use to check the status with DescribeStatement and retrieve results with GetStatementResult. The maximum query result size is 100 MB.

variable.description: AWS region identifier (This can only be one of us-east-1,us-east-2,us-west-1,us-west-2,eu-west-1,eu-west-2,eu-central-1,ap-southeast-1,ap-southeast-2,ap-northeast-1,ap-northeast-2,ca-central-1,sa-east-1)

AWS region identifier (This can only be one of us-east-1,us-east-2,us-west-1,us-west-2,eu-west-1,eu-west-2,eu-central-1,ap-southeast-1,ap-southeast-2,ap-northeast-1,ap-northeast-2,ca-central-1,sa-east-1)

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

body.raw:
{
  "Database": "dev",
  "Sql": "SELECT * FROM sales ORDER BY sale_date DESC LIMIT 10",
  "ClusterIdentifier": "my-redshift-cluster",
  "DbUser": "example_value",
  "SecretArn": "arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:123456789012:secret:my-redshift-secret-AbCdEf",
  "StatementName": "my-sales-query",
  "WithEvent": true,
  "WorkgroupName": "my-workgroup",
  "ClientToken": "example_value"
}

item.name: Amazon Redshift Execute Multiple Sql Statements in a Batch

request.name: Amazon Redshift Execute Multiple Sql Statements in a Batch

description.content: Runs one or more SQL statements as a batch, which run as a single transaction. The statements run serially in the order provided. Returns a statement identifier for the batch that you can use with DescribeStatement and GetStatementResult.

variable.description: AWS region identifier (This can only be one of us-east-1,us-east-2,us-west-1,us-west-2,eu-west-1,eu-west-2,eu-central-1,ap-southeast-1,ap-southeast-2,ap-northeast-1,ap-northeast-2,ca-central-1,sa-east-1)

item.name: Amazon Redshift List Sql Statement Executions

request.name: Amazon Redshift List Sql Statement Executions

description.content: Lists SQL statements that have been submitted. By default, only finished statements are shown. You can filter by status and optionally include statements submitted by all IAM users by setting RoleLevel to true. Results are ordered by creation time, with the most recent statements listed first.

variable.description: AWS region identifier (This can only be one of us-east-1,us-east-2,us-west-1,us-west-2,eu-west-1,eu-west-2,eu-central-1,ap-southeast-1,ap-southeast-2,ap-northeast-1,ap-northeast-2,ca-central-1,sa-east-1)

AWS region identifier (This can only be one of us-east-1,us-east-2,us-west-1,us-west-2,eu-west-1,eu-west-2,eu-central-1,ap-southeast-1,ap-southeast-2,ap-northeast-1,ap-northeast-2,ca-central-1,sa-east-1)

The Amazon Redshift Data API enables you to run SQL statements on Amazon Redshift clusters and Amazon Redshift Serverless workgroups without managing database connections or drivers. The Data API handles connection management, authentication via IAM or Secrets Manager, and supports asynchronous execution of SQL statements with result retrieval through a statement identifier. Ideal for building serverless applications, integrating with AWS Lambda, and running ad-hoc queries programmatically.
